page_content:
ENPM691 Hacking of C programs and Unix Binaries (3 Credits) Teaches the fundamentals of secure programming in C. An in depth discussion on various security vulnerabilities (e.g., buffer overflows) in C applications will be taught with hands-on demo of concepts during the class. Students will learn how a C program runs "under-the-hood". The course will teach nitty-gritty of C programs by analyzing at the assembly level. The course discusses best practices (e.g., coding standards) and design principles for secure programming so that security can be built-in during design time. In addition to assignments, students are required to present papers related to this course. Prerequisite: ENEE150 or equivalent.

ENPM692 Manufacturing and Automation (3 Credits) Covers automation and product realization, digital factories, and disruptive manufacturing technologies. The role of additive manufacturing, sustainability, and performance simulation in selected manufacturing scenarios will be explored alongside automation strategies for rapid product development. Credit Only Granted for: ENPM808P or ENPM692. Formerly: ENPM808P.
